content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Reviewed the PID liveness safety changes across platforms. The diff contains two substantive code changes (excluding .ralph-burning/ orchestration state): (1) In src/cli/run.rs:2181, `is_pid_alive` is replaced with `pid_record_matches_live_process` in the interrupted-handoff cleanup path, correctly handling legacy PID records without proc_start_ticks/proc_start_marker by falling back to live-process start time comparison. (2) In tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs, the timeout test is relaxed to accept either Timeout or TransportFailure, accommodating nix sandbox environments where teardown confirmation fails. A new Linux-only regression test validates the legacy PID path. All remaining is_pid_alive call sites were verified: they are either guarded by pid_record_is_authoritative() checks, operate in stop/signal flows where conservative behavior is correct, or use freshly-captured identity fields. The legacy_pid_record_matches_live_process fallback logic is sound — it checks PID liveness, retrieves the live process start time, and compares against the recorded started_at to detect PID reuse. The clean timeout path remains strictly tested by the separate clean_timeout_teardown_yields_timeout_without_warning test. No bugs, safety problems, or correctness gaps found.

## Amendments

None.
